---
title: "Working with DERA Notes data using Polars"
format:
  html:
    colorlinks: true
    fig-format: png
engine: jupyter
jupyter: notes
---
The source code for this note can be found [here](https://github.com/iangow/notes/blob/main/published/dera-polars.qmd).
The package `era_pl` is installed using `pip install 'era-py[polars]'`.

```{python}
import polars as pl
from era_pl import load_parquet
```

There are three Parquet files that I use here:

 - [`sub_notes_2024q1`](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/qnwav4kdjugwpw6svuogk/sub_notes_2024q1.parquet?rlkey=oaerjel2y3juoducsh0pyerkb&dl=0)
 - [`num_notes_2024q1`](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/nruobijayw1tf1zjp5jbe/num_notes_2024_01.parquet?rlkey=6z5tz8ww7j52bjljxf6y4m4j6&dl=0)
 - [`dim_notes_2024q1`](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/i0vejw0ykdzdae0rfwp1w/dim_notes_2024q1.parquet?rlkey=2h4lqawuzo8i0wyqsssh253nm&dl=0)

```{python}
submissions = load_parquet('sub_notes_2024q1', 'dera_notes')
numericFacts = load_parquet('num_notes_2024q1', 'dera_notes')
dimensions = load_parquet('dim_notes_2024q1', 'dera_notes')
```

The commands above are equivalent to the following if you put the data files in your working directory:

```{python}
#| eval: false
submissions = pl.scan_parquet('sub_notes_2024q1.parquet')
numericFacts = pl.scan_parquet('num_notes_2024q1.parquet')
dimensions = pl.scan_parquet('dim_notes_2024q1.parquet')
```

I use the former form because I have organized my Parquet data repository along the lines outlined [here](https://iangow.github.io/era_pl_book/parquet-wrds.html) or [here](https://iangow.github.io/far_book/parquet-wrds.html#sec-make-pq).

```{python}
submissions.head().collect()
```

```{python}
numericFacts.head().collect()
```

```{python}
dimensions.head().collect()
```

```{python}
%%time
mergedDF = (
    submissions
    .join(numericFacts, on='adsh')
    .join(dimensions, left_on='dimh', right_on='dimhash')
    .filter(pl.col("name") == 'DELTA AIR LINES, INC.',
            pl.col("tag") == 'RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax',
            pl.col("ddate") == pl.date(2023, 12, 31),
            pl.col("qtrs") == 4,
            pl.col("iprx") == 0)
    .select('name', 'dimn', 'segments', 'uom', 'value')
    .sort('dimn', 'segments')
    .collect()
)
```

```{python}
mergedDF.style
```
